findings.finding: Requirement 6's term-already-exists probe reads only cortex/requirements/glossary.md — a candidate parked in spec Open Decisions has no glossary entry yet, probe cannot deduplicate against prior deferred definitions.
findings.evidence_quote: on resolving a term inline, (a) reads cortex/requirements/glossary.md if it exists, (b) if the term is already present: uses the existing entry verbatim or surfaces the conflict to the user before reclassifying

findings.finding: Requirement 5 narrows requirements-gather's no-filesystem contract for glossary writes and explicitly excludes glossary from lazy artifact creation, but does not specify the semantics of partial glossary state when a user abandons mid-interview after some terms have been appended.
findings.evidence_quote: Lazy artifact creation still applies to project.md and area docs — the Q&A block is held in conversation context until /requirements-write synthesizes

findings.finding: Requirement 15 specifies the four-bucket classifier applies at skill-edit time and at glossary-audit time, but the spec defines no glossary-audit surface anywhere — no skill, no requirement, no event invokes one. The advisory doc has no consumer.
findings.evidence_quote: it applies at skill-edit time and at glossary-audit time, NOT at inline-write time

findings.finding: Req 4's embed-in-step-1 alternative silently redefines step 1 from a single unconditional file load to a doc-parse-and-multi-load; only the rename-and-rescope branch of Req 4 is clean. Downgraded A→B because the requirement offers a clean alternative.
findings.evidence_quote: Either rename and rescope the test to match a new step count, or preserve the five-step shape by embedding the Global Context read inside step 1 rather than adding a new step.

findings.finding: Req 3 introduces a silent-skip-on-absence semantic for Global Context entries but does not update step 4's 'Record the full list of loaded requirements files' clause to specify whether absent entries are recorded as attempted-but-skipped or omitted entirely; downstream drift-check and reviewer-dispatch consumers cited in step 4 have no documented contract for that distinction.
findings.evidence_quote: silently skipping any listed file that does not exist on disk

findings.finding: Req 1 positions ## Global Context as a structural peer of ## Conditional Loading, but the Matching Semantics sub-section of load-requirements.md documents only Conditional Loading's substring-match-against-phrase semantics; the spec does not propose adding a Global Context counterpart describing the list-of-paths semantic.

Requirement 11's Language-section-only read at critical-review Step 2a filters by section name but not by section content; the spec does not constrain what producers write into ## Language, so four-bucket reasoning can leak into the critical-review Project Context block by being encoded into Language entries themselves.
findings.evidence_quote: Do NOT read the ## Relationships, ## Example dialogue, or ## Flagged ambiguities sections — those could approach 'existing reasoning' territory.

findings.finding: Open Decisions defers advisory-doc location to implementation time while Non-Requirements asserts the boundary holds regardless; the location is load-bearing for that Non-Requirement (cortex/requirements/ placement co-locates with the producer's read surface; docs/skills/ does not), so the deferral leaves a durability-relevant choice outside spec review.
findings.evidence_quote: Advisory-doc location: cortex/requirements/glossary-classifier.md (lives alongside the glossary it advises) or docs/skills/glossary-classifier.md (lives where skill-author guidance lives).
